mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing starts rate above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 105 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces the efficiency of production and boosts costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the employment rate below 145 million signals a weakening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 2.5% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 2.5% and a 3-month decrease in the industrial production index below 105 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts production costs and reduces efficiency, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.
